tasks.title: Add PID recycling edge case comment to tryCleanStaleLock
tasks.description: Add PID recycling edge case comment to tryCleanStaleLock
tasks.body: Add an inline comment to the tryCleanStaleLock function in internal/state/filelock.go explaining the PID recycling edge case. The comment should explain: (1) if the original lock holder dies and a new process is assigned the same PID within the 50ms polling window, signalProcess will return nil (process alive), so stale detection will incorrectly conclude the lock is still held; (2) the consequence is a timeout waiting for the lock, not data corruption, because flock serialization still protects the file. Place the comment near the signalProcess call (around line 106-109) where the false-alive determination happens. Keep it concise, a few lines at most.
acceptance_criteria: tryCleanStaleLock contains a comment explaining the PID recycling race: same-PID reuse within the polling window causes a false-alive reading

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in add-comment is complete and correct

breadcrumbs.text: Added PID recycling edge case comment to tryCleanStaleLock in internal/state/filelock.go

Audit reviewed 1 file (internal/state/filelock.go, 128 lines). PASS. The change is a four-line comment above signalProcess in tryCleanStaleLock (lines 106-109) documenting the PID recycling edge case. Comment is technically accurate: PID reuse within the polling window causes a false-alive signal, leading to timeout rather than corruption because flock serializes access. No code changes, no new risk surfaces. Build and tests pass. Pre-existing discarded errors in Acquire (Truncate, Seek, Fprintf, Sync) and tryCleanStaleLock (Seek) are outside this node's scope. Quality: clean, well-placed documentation that says exactly what the reader needs to know, right where they need it.

audit.result_summary: Added a concise comment to tryCleanStaleLock in internal/state/filelock.go documenting the PID recycling edge case: when a lock holder dies and another process inherits its PID within the polling window, signalProcess returns nil (alive), causing a timeout rather than corruption because flock still serializes access. The comment is accurate, well-placed, and audit-verified against the surrounding code.

task-0001.objective: Document the PID recycling edge case in tryCleanStaleLock
task-0001.what_happened: Added a four-line comment above the signalProcess call in internal/state/filelock.go explaining that PID reuse within the polling window causes a false-alive reading, resulting in timeout rather than corruption thanks to flock serialization.
went_well: Comment is concise and placed exactly where the reader needs it
improvements: None; this was a documentation-only change
